questions.question: Why would a memory dump be admissible as evidence in court?

options.A: Because it is used to identify the state of the system.
options.B: Because it is used to demonstrate the truth of the contents.
options.C: Because the state of the memory cannot be used as evidence.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Because it is used to identify the state of the system.. The other options are incorrect: B (Because it is used to demonstrate the truth of the contents.); C (Because the state of the memory cannot be used as evidence.); D (Because of the exclusionary rule.).

questions.question: Which type of attack would a competitive intelligence attack best classify as?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Business attack. The other options are incorrect: B (Grudge attack); C (Intelligence attack); D (Financial attack).

questions.question: Once an intrusion into your organization's information system has been detected, which of the following actions should be performed first?

options.A: Determine to what extent systems and data are compromised.
options.C: Eliminate all means of intruder access.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Determine to what extent systems and data are compromised.. The other options are incorrect: B (Communicate with relevant parties.); C (Eliminate all means of intruder access.); D (Contain the).

questions.question: When first analyzing an intrusion that hasjust been detected and confirmingthat it is a true positive, which of the following actions should be done as a first step ifyou wish to prosecute the attacker in court?

options.B: Identify the attacks used to gain access.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Back up the compromised systems.. The other options are incorrect: B (Identify the attacks used to gain access.); C (Capture and record system); D (Isolate the compromised systems.).

questions.question: In orderto be able to successfully prosecute an intruder

options.A: Whenever possible, analyze a replica of the compromised resource, not the original, thereby avoiding inadvertentlytamping with evidence.
options.B: Collection ofevidence has to be done following predefined procedures
options.C: A proper chain ofcustody ofevidence has to be preserved.
options.D: A point ofcontact should be designated to be responsible for communicating with law enforcement and other external agencies.

questions.question: In orderto preserve a proper chain of custody of evidence

options.A: Log files containing information regarding an intrusion are retained for at least as long as normal business records, and longer in the case of an ongoing
options.B: Verifiable documentation indicating the sequence of individuals who have handled a piece of evidence should be available.
options.D: Evidence has to be collected following predefined procedures in accordance with all laws and legal regulations.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Verifiable documentation indicating the sequence of individuals who have handled a piece of evidence should be available.. The other options are incorrect: A (Log files containing information regarding an intrusion are retained for at least as long as normal business records, and longer in the case of an ongoing); C (Law enforcement); D (Evidence has to be collected following predefined procedures in accordance with all laws and legal regulations.).

questions.question: Which of the following should be notified first in the event of a Network or System
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: lnternal public relations point ofcontact. The other options are incorrect: A (Human resources); B (System and network administrator(s)); D (Law enforcement agencies).

questions.question: When should a post-mortem review meeting be held after an intrusion has been properlytaken care of?

options.A: Explain what is considered an acceptable use of the e-mail system.
options.B: Explain who can read the e-mail and how long it is backed up.
options.C: lnform all employees that e-mail is being monitored.
options.D: Monitor only a limited number of employees.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Monitor only a limited number of employees.. The other options are incorrect: A (Explain what is considered an acceptable use of the e-mail system.); B (Explain who can read the e-mail and how long it is backed up.); C (lnform all employees that e-mail is being monitored.).

options.B: The 1996 U.S. National Information Infrastructure Protection Act
options.D: The 1986 U.S. Computer Fraud and Abuse Act

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: The 1991 U.S. The other options are incorrect: A (The 1987 U.S); B (The 1996 U.S. National Information Infrastructure Protection Act); D (The 1986 U.S. Computer Fraud and Abuse Act).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Destruction. The other options are incorrect: B (Storage, preservation, and transportation); C (Acquisition collection and); D (Analysis).

options.B: Computer-generated records are only considered secondary evidence, thus are not as reliable as best evidence.
options.C: In many instances, an expert or specialist is not required.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Computer-generated records are only considered secondary evidence, thus are not as reliable as best evidence.. The other options are incorrect: A (Evidence is easyto gather.); C (In many instances, an expert or specialist is not required.); D (Information is tangible.).

questions.question: Which of the following exceptions is less likelyto make evidence such as audit logs and audittrails admissible and have more weight in court under the Business Exemption Rule to the hearsay evidence

options.A: Records are collected during the regular conduct of business.
options.B: Records are collected at or near the time ofoccurrence of the act being investigated to generate automated reports.
options.C: You can prove no one could have changed the records/data/logs that were collected.
options.D: Records are collected by senior or executive management.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Records are collected by senior or executive management.. questions.question: Which of the following is the BESTway to detect software license violations?

options.B: Regularly scanning PCs in use to ensure that unauthorized copies of software have not been loaded on the PC.
options.C: Installing metering software on the LAN so applications can be accessed through the
options.D: Implementing a corporate policy on copyright infringements and software use.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Regularly scanning PCs in use to ensure that unauthorized copies of software have not been loaded on the PC.. The other options are incorrect: A (Requiringthat all PCs be diskless workstations.); C (Installing metering software on the LAN so applications can be accessed through the); D (Implementing a corporate policy on copyright infringements and software use.).

questions.question: Which of the following best defines a Computer Security Incident Response Team

options.A: An organization that ensures that security incidents are reported to the authorities.
options.B: An organization that coordinates and supports the response to security
options.C: An organization that disseminates incident-related information to its constituency and other involved parties.
options.D: An organization that provides a secure channel for receiving reports about suspected

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: An organization that coordinates and supports the response to security. The other options are incorrect: A (An organization that ensures that security incidents are reported to the authorities.); C (An organization that disseminates incident-related information to its constituency and other involved parties.); D (An organization that provides a secure channel for receiving reports about suspected).

questions.question: Underthe principle of culpable negligence, executives can be held liable for losses that result from computer system breaches if

options.A: They have failed to properly insure computer resources against
options.B: They have not exercised due care protecting computing resources.
options.C: The company is not a multi-national company.
options.D: The company does not prosecute the hacker that caused the breach.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: They have not exercised due care protecting computing resources.. The other options are incorrect: A (They have failed to properly insure computer resources against); C (The company is not a multi-national company.); D (The company does not prosecute the hacker that caused the breach.).

options.B: Confidentiality of data transfered between European and international companies

questions.question: What Cloud Deployment model consist of a cloud infrastructure provisioned for exclusive use by a single organization comprising multiple consumers (e.g., business units)? questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Private Cloud. The other options are incorrect: B (Community Cloud HIXYI); C (PubHc(]oud); D (Hybrid Cloud).

questions.question: When referring to the Cloud Computing Service models. What would you call a service model where the consumer does not manage or control the underlying cloud infrastructure including networks, servers, operating systems, or storage, but has control overthe deployed applications and possibly configuration settings for the application-hosting environment?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Platform as a Service (PaaS). The other options are incorrect: A (Code as a Service (CaaS)); B (Infrastructure as a Service (laaS)); D (Software as a Service (SaaS)).

questions.question: Who should direct short-term recovery actions immediately following a disaster?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Disaster Recovery Manager.. The other options are incorrect: A (ChiefOperating Officer.); B (Chief Information Officer.); C (Chief Executive Officer.).

questions.question: Prior to a live disaster test also called a Full Interruption test, which of the following is most important?

options.A: Conduct of a successful Parallel Test
options.D: Arrange physical securityfor the test site.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Conduct of a successful Parallel Test. The other options are incorrect: B (Restore all); C (Document expected); D (Arrange physical securityfor the test site.).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Dependencies. The other options are incorrect: A (Priorities); C (Service); D (Composition ZHEYL.).

options.D: elapsed time to perform various activities.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: list ofsuccessful and unsuccessful activities.. The other options are incorrect: A (amount ofwork completed.); B (description ofeach activity.); D (elapsed time to perform various activities.).

questions.question: Which of the following computer recovery sites is only partially equipped with processing equipment?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: warm site.. The other options are incorrect: A (hot site.); B (cold site.); D (rolling hot site.).

questions.question: Which of the following computer recovery sites is the least expensive and the most difficult to test?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: cold site.. The other options are incorrect: A (non-mobile hot site.); C (warm site.); D (mobile hot site.).

questions.question: Which of the following is the most important consideration in locating an alternate computing facility during the development of a disaster recovery plan?

options.A: It is close enough to become operational quickly.
options.B: It is convenient to airports and hotels.
options.C: It is unlikely to be affected by the same contingency.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: It is unlikely to be affected by the same contingency.. The other options are incorrect: A (It is close enough to become operational quickly.); B (It is convenient to airports and hotels.).

questions.question: Contracts and agreements are often times unenforceable or hard to enforce in which of the following alternate facility recovery agreement?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: reciprocal agreement.. The other options are incorrect: A (hot site.); B (cold site.); D (warm site.).

questions.question: Organizations should not view disaster recovery as which of the following?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Discretionary expense.. The other options are incorrect: A (Compliance with regulations.); B (Committed expense.); D (Enforcement of legal statutes.).

questions.question: Which of the following backup sites is the most effective for disaster recovery?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Hot sites. The other options are incorrect: A (Reciprocal Agreement); B (Cold sites); C (Time brokers).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a transaction redundancy implementation?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: on-site mirroring. The other options are incorrect: A (database shadowing); B (remote journaling El); C (electronic vaulting).

questions.question: Which of the following provides enterprise management with a prioritized list of time-critical business processes, and estimates a recovery time objective for each of the time critical processes and the components of the enterprise that support those processess?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Business Impact Assessment. The other options are incorrect: A (Current State Assessment); B (Business Risk Assessment.); D (Risk Mitigation Assessment.).

questions.question: Which of the following steps is NOT one of the eight detailed steps of a Business Impact Assessment (BIA)

options.A: Calculating the risk for each different business
options.D: Notifying senior management of the start of the assessment.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Notifying senior management of the start of the assessment.. The other options are incorrect: A (Calculating the risk for each different business); B (Identifying critical business); C (Creating data gathering techniques.).

questions.question: Which of the following results in the most devastating business interruptions?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Loss of Data. The other options are incorrect: A (Loss of Hardwar ie i/S); C (Loss of Communication Links); D (Loss ofApplications).

questions.question: During recovery from a disaster, which offollowing is the most critical item?
Which of the following is defined as the most recent point in time to which data must be synchronized without adversely affecting the organization (financial or operational impacts)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Recovery Point Objective Tit. The other options are incorrect: B (Critical Time Objective); C (Point ofTime Objective); D (Recovery Time Objective).

questions.question: The Recovery Point Objective (RPO) in Disaster Recovery Planning refers to which of the following?

options.A: Point to which information system must be operational at alternate site
options.B: Point to which application data must be recovered to resume business transactions
options.C: Maximum elapsed time required to complete recovery of application data
options.D: Point to which application data must be recovered to resume system operations

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Point to which application data must be recovered to resume business transactions. The other options are incorrect: A (Point to which information system must be operational at alternate site); C (Maximum elapsed time required to complete recovery of application data); D (Point to which application data must be recovered to resume system operations).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Money and Securities. The other options are incorrect: A (lnscribed, printed and Written documents); C (Accounts Receivable); D (Manuscripts).
